reasoning: The threshold requires more than 10 times the baseline rate of protests in a 30-day window, which means roughly 342 protests in a single month. For the first resolution date (2026-02-08), 23 days of the 30-day window have already passed with no reports in international or specialized media (like OVD-Info or ACLED updates) of any significant protest wave in Russia in January 2026. With fewer than 7 days remaining, reaching 342 protests is practically impossible. For the second resolution date (2026-03-03), the window covers February 2026. While February 24 marks the anniversary of the invasion of Ukraine and has historically seen isolated protests, Russia's severe repression of anti-war sentiment and the strict ACLED definition of protests (requiring 3+ people, thereby excluding single-person pickets) drastically limits the frequency of recorded protests. Hitting a 10x surge is a black swan event requiring a massive catalyst (like a regime collapse or large-scale mobilization), for which there are currently no indicators. Thus, probabilities are set at the minimum allowable thresholds, slightly increasing for the latter date to reflect the marginally wider time horizon.
